revision.comment: Text replacement - " *" to "**"
revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = 101127 Uji-jinja Uji Kyoto pref Japan01s3.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.89106|135.81069|type:landmark_region:JP|format=dms|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | image = [[File:Uji-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Chūmon (Middle Gate) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}}|34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子}} | rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ({{ill|Uji Shrine|ja|宇治神社}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and was ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社}}. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子}}
（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|ja|京阪宇治線}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Buildings in Uji City]] [[Category:History of Uji City]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties]] [[Category:National Parks of Lake Biwa]] [[ja:宇治神社]] == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]][[Category:11 3]][[en:Uji Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Uji Shrine]][[Category:11]][[Category:Uji Shrine]][[Category:11]] Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|ja|京阪宇治線}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} [[Category:Buildings in Uji City]] [[Category:History of Uji City]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of It is a designated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and was ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社}}. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子}}
（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several Chūmon (Middle Gate) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}}|34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子}} | rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ({{ill|Uji Shrine|ja|宇治神社}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} and was ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社}}. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子}}
（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|ja|京阪宇治線}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = 101127 Uji-jinja Uji Kyoto pref Japan01s3.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.89106|135.81069|type:landmark_region:JP|format=dms|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | image = [[File:Uji-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Chūmon (Middle Gate) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|12=draft}}|34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=draft}} | rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ({{ill|Uji Shrine|ja|宇治神社|12=draft}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} and was ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|12=draft}}. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=draft}}
（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実|12=draft}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=draft}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|12=draft}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|12=draft}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|12=draft}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|ja|京阪宇治線|12=draft}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|12=draft}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|12=draft}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|12=draft}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|12=draft}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|ja|京阪宇治線|12=draft}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|12=draft}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = 101127 Uji-jinja Uji Kyoto pref Japan01s3.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.89106|135.81069|type:landmark_region:JP|format=dms|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | image = [[File:Uji-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Chūmon (Middle Gate) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|12=draft|12=draft}}|34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=draft|12=draft}} | rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ({{ill|Uji Shrine|ja|宇治神社|12=draft|12=draft}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and was ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=draft|12=draft}}
（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実|12=draft|12=draft}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|12=draft|12=draft}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|ja|京阪宇治線|12=draft|12=draft}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|12=draft|12=draft}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings in Uji City]] [[Category:History of Uji City]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties]] [[Category:National Parks of Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]] Shrine]] [[ja:宇治神社]] [[en:Uji Shrine]] [[zh:宇治神社]] [[de:Uji-Schrein]] [[fr:Uji-jinja]] [[ro:Uji (sanctuar)]]

It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|12=draft|12=draft}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|ja|京阪宇治線|12=draft|12=draft}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|12=draft|12=draft}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = 101127 Uji-jinja Uji Kyoto pref Japan01s3.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.89106|135.81069|type:landmark_region:JP|format=dms|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | image = [[File:Uji-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Chūmon (Middle Gate) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|12=draft|12=draft}}|34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=draft|12=draft}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ({{ill|Uji Shrine|ja|宇治神社|12=draft|12=draft}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=draft|12=draft}}
（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実|12=draft|12=draft}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|12=draft|12=draft}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|ja|京阪宇治線|12=draft|12=draft}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|12=draft|12=draft}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{draft categories| }} 宇治神社 | image = 101127 Uji-jinja Uji Kyoto pref Japan01s3.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.89106|135.81069|type:landmark_region:JP|format=dms|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | image = [[File:Uji-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Chūmon (Middle Gate) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ({{ill|Uji Shrine|ja|宇治神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|ja|京阪宇治線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings in Uji City]] [[Category:History of Uji City]] [[Category:Shikinai shrines of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties]] [[Category:National Parks of Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uji Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uji Shrine]] [[Category:前賢故実]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uji Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Uji Shrine]] [[ja:宇治神社]] [[en:Uji Shrine]] [[zh:宇治神社]] [[de:Uji-Schrein]] [[fr:Uji-jinja]] [[ro:Uji (sanctuar)]] revision.comment: 51 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:宇治神社 up to rev 100017465
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 宇治神社 |画像 = [[File:Uji-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
中門 |所在地 = [[京都府]][[宇治市]]宇治山田1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=宇治神社}} |祭神 = [[菟道稚郎子|菟道稚郎子命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[府社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]] |別名 = 離宮明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[5月8日]]・[[6月8日]] |神事 = }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''宇治神社'''（うじじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[宇治市]]宇治山田にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[府社]]。隣接する[[宇治上神社]]とは対をなす。 == 祭神 == [[ファイル:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|[[菟道稚郎子]]
（『[[前賢故実]]』）}}]] 祭神は次の1柱。 * '''[[菟道稚郎子|菟道稚郎子命]]'''（うじのわきいらつこのみこと） *: 『日本書紀』では「菟道稚郎子」、『古事記』では「宇遅之和紀郎子」と表記される。第15代[[応神天皇]]の皇子。 *: 応神天皇は、菟道稚郎子命を皇嗣と定められ、菟道稚郎子命の兄宮である大鶴鷯命（後の[[仁徳天皇]]）を舗導にあてられた。これは、古代の慣例で、年が若い者に嗣がせたほうが一代の活躍期間が長く、より国家の繁栄が期待できるとされていたからである。ところが、菟道稚郎子命は、[[阿直岐]]や[[王仁]]を師に迎え、典籍に通じていたことから長男相続説を守っておられており、応神天皇崩御後に兄の大鶴鷯命に皇位に就くよう勧められた。しかし、大鶴鷯命がこれを拒否したことから皇位の譲り合いが三年程続いた。その間、菟道稚郎子命は宮居を菟道に移され、最終的に皇位を早く定めて天下の煩いを除くためにと自害され、大鶴鷯命に皇位を譲られたのである宇治神社の案内看板より。。 *: 本殿には、菟道稚郎子像と伝える神像（国の[[重要文化財]]）が祀られている。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]で「宇治神社二座」と見える2座のうち1座に比定される。この「二座」を祭神と見た場合、菟道稚郎子を1座とすることは動かないものの、もう1座については父の応神天皇・異母兄の仁徳天皇・母の矢河枝比売とする諸説がある『日本の神々』宇治上神社・宇治神社項。。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 創建年代などの起源は明らかではない。宇治神社のすぐ近くには[[宇治上神社]]があるが（{{Coord|34|53|31.44|N|135|48|41.18|E|region:JP-26|name=宇治上神社}}）、宇治神社とは二社一体の存在であった。 宇治上神社は、第15代[[応神天皇|応身天皇]]の離宮「桐原日桁宮」の旧跡であり、皇子の[[菟道稚郎子]]命の宮居跡と伝えられており、菟道稚郎子命の薨御後に御神霊を祀ったのが始まりである。 宇治上神社の境内は『山城国風土記』に見える菟道稚郎子の離宮「桐原日桁宮」の旧跡であると伝え、両社旧称の「離宮明神」もそれに因むといわれる。 宇治上神社の境内には「天降石」・「岩神さん」と呼ばれる巨石があり、[[磐境]]信仰による創祀という説もある。 === 概史 === [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[山城国]][[宇治郡]]に「宇治神社二座 鍬靫」の記載があるが、その2座はそれぞれ宇治神社・宇治上神社に比定される『京都府の地名』宇治上神社項・宇治神社項。。神名帳の「鍬靫」の記載は、祈年祭の際に朝廷から鍬・靫の奉献があったことを意味する。後に近くに[[平等院]]ができると、両社はその[[鎮守社]]とされたという。 [[明治]]以前は宇治神社は「下社」・「若宮」、宇治上神社は「上社」・「本宮」と呼ばれたほか、両社を合わせて「宇治離宮明神」・「宇治離宮八幡宮」と総称された。 明治に入って宇治上神社とは分離し、[[1911年]]（明治44年）[[府社]]に昇格した。 [[2018年]]（[[平成]]30年）には[[平成30年台風第21号|台風第21号]]で大鳥居が土台部分から折れて倒壊する被害に遭った{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.mbs.jp/news/kansainews/20180905/GE000000000000024232.shtml|title=台風21号被害　京都・宇治神社で大鳥居が土台部分から折れる|date=2018-09-05|accessdate=2018-09-05|publisher=毎日放送|archivedate=2018-09-06|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20180906135346/https://www.mbs.jp/news/kansainews/20180905/GE000000000000024232.shtml}}。 === 神使いの兎 === 菟道稚郎子命が、河内の国から菟道へ向かわれる途中、道に迷われた。その際、一羽のうさぎが現れ、後からついてこられる命を先導したという古伝から「みかえりうさぎ」といわれ、神使とされている。 現在、宇治神社ではみかえり兎の絵馬やおみくじを頒布しており、毎月一日のみ、ブロンズのみかえりうさぎの置物が現れる{{Cite web|和書|url=http://uji-jinja.com/event/150403usagi.html |title=〜願掛け　うさぎさん巡り、「みかえり兎」おみくじ〜 |access-date=2023-08-06 |publisher=宇治神社}}。 == 境内 == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|本殿（重要文化財）}}]] * 本殿（[[重要文化財]]） - 三間社[[流造]]、[[檜皮葺]]。[[鎌倉時代]]後期の造営とされる。 * 中門 * 拝殿（桐原殿、京都府指定有形文化財） - 入母屋造、桁行3間、梁間3間、檜皮葺。 * 絵馬堂 * 神楽殿 * 神輿蔵 * 社務所 - 2階は参集殿になっている。 * 飛び地境内 ** [[御旅所]]（京都府宇治市宇治1、{{ウィキ座標|34|53|16.91|N|135|47|55.73|E|region:JP-26|位置|name=御旅所}}） File:Uji-jinja, keidai.jpg|境内
{{small|左奥に本殿、右に拝殿（桐原殿）。}} File:Uji-jinja, keidai-torii.jpg|中門前の鳥居 File:Uji-jinja, Kirihara-den.jpg|拝殿（桐原殿） == 摂末社 == * 春日社（京都府指定有形文化財） - 祭神：[[タケミカヅチ|建甕槌命]]、[[フツヌシ|斎主命]]、[[天児屋根命]]。室町時代築。 * 日吉神社（京都府指定有形文化財） - 祭神：[[大山咋命]] * 住吉神社（京都府指定有形文化財） - 祭神：[[住吉三神]]（底筒男命、中筒男命、上筒男命）、[[神功皇后]] * 廣田神社 - 祭神：[[ヒルコ|蛭子命]] * 松尾神社 - 祭神：[[市杵島姫命]] * 高良神社 - 祭神：[[武内宿禰]] * 伊勢両宮社 - 祭神：[[天照皇大神]]、[[国常立命]] ファイル:Uji-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|境内社
画像右から
春日神社
日吉神社
住吉神社 ファイル:Uji-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|境内社
画像左から

伊勢両宮社 == 祭事 ==
年間祭事一覧
* 毎月 ** 月次祭 （毎月1日） * 1月 ** 歳旦祭 （[[1月1日]]） * 2月 ** 節分祭 （[[2月3日]]） * 4月 ** 水神祭 （4月第2日曜日） * 5月 ** 例祭（神幸祭） （[[5月8日]]） * 6月 ** 幣渡祭 （[[6月5日]]） ** 例祭（還幸祭・離宮祭） （[[6月8日]]） ** 大祓式 （[[6月30日]]） * 10月 ** 献茶祭 （[[10月1日]]） * 12月 ** 火焚祭 （12月中旬の日曜日） ** 大祓式 （[[12月31日]]）
== 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 本殿 1棟（建造物） *: [[鎌倉時代]]後期の造営。[[1902年]]（[[明治]]35年）7月31日に[[古社寺保存法]]に基づき特別保護建造物に指定、[[1950年]]（[[昭和]]25年）の[[文化財保護法]]施行により重要文化財に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|1949|宇治神社本殿}}。 * 木造莵道稚郎子命坐像 1軀（彫刻） *: [[平安時代]]の作。[[1903年]]（明治36年）4月15日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|3643|木造莵道稚郎子命坐像}}。 === 京都府指定有形文化財 === * 拝殿 1棟（建造物） * 末社春日社本殿 1棟（建造物） - [[2018年]]（[[平成]]30年）3月23日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_2965_1.pdf 平成30年3月23日京都府公報]}}より京都府教育委員会告示第3号（リンクは京都府ホームページ）。。 * 末社日吉社本殿 1棟（建造物） * 末社住吉社本殿 1棟（建造物） === 宇治市指定有形文化財 === * 白色尉面（雪掻きの面） 1面（彫刻） - [[安土桃山時代]]の作。[[1972年]]（[[昭和]]47年）3月1日指定[https://www.city.uji.kyoto.jp/0000004257.html 宇治市の文化財]（宇治市ホームページ）。。 * 木造狛犬 2躯（彫刻） - 鎌倉時代の作。宇治市歴史資料館に寄託。[[1988年]]（昭和63年）3月31日指定。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[京都府]][[宇治市]]宇治山田1 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道 ** [[京阪電気鉄道]]（京阪電鉄）[[京阪宇治線|宇治線]] [[宇治駅 (京阪)|宇治駅]] （徒歩5分） ** [[西日本旅客鉄道]]（JR西日本）[[JR奈良線|奈良線]] [[宇治駅 (JR西日本)|宇治駅]] （徒歩10分） '''周辺''' * [[宇治上神社]] * [[平等院]] * 菟道稚郎子尊 宇治墓 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『[[日本歴史地名大系]] 京都府の地名』（[[平凡社]]）宇治市 宇治上神社項・宇治神社項 * 山路興造「宇治上神社・宇治神社」（[[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 5 山城・近江』（[[白水社]]、1986年） ISBN 978-4560025055） == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Uji-jinja}} * [http://uji-jinja.com/ 宇治神社] - 公式サイト *[https://www.kyotoside.jp/entry/20210306 京都府公式メディア（宇治神社）] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うししんしや}} [[Category:宇治市の建築物]] [[Category:宇治市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:宇治市の重要文化財]] [[Category:京都府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:京都府の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]] [[Category:琵琶湖国定公園]] [[Category:日本遺産]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[宇治神社]] to [[Uji Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 宇治神社 |画像 = [[File:Uji-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
中門 |所在地 = [[京都府]][[宇治市]]宇治山田1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=宇治神社}} |祭神 = [[菟道稚郎子|菟道稚郎子命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[府社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]] |別名 = 離宮明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[5月8日]]・[[6月8日]] |神事 = }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''宇治神社'''（うじじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[宇治市]]宇治山田にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[府社]]。隣接する[[宇治上神社]]とは対をなす。 == 祭神 == [[ファイル:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|[[菟道稚郎子]]
（『[[前賢故実]]』）}}]] 祭神は次の1柱。 * '''[[菟道稚郎子|菟道稚郎子命]]'''（うじのわきいらつこのみこと） *: 『日本書紀』では「菟道稚郎子」、『古事記』では「宇遅之和紀郎子」と表記される。第15代[[応神天皇]]の皇子。 *: 応神天皇は、菟道稚郎子命を皇嗣と定められ、菟道稚郎子命の兄宮である大鶴鷯命（後の[[仁徳天皇]]）を舗導にあてられた。これは、古代の慣例で、年が若い者に嗣がせたほうが一代の活躍期間が長く、より国家の繁栄が期待できるとされていたからである。ところが、菟道稚郎子命は、[[阿直岐]]や[[王仁]]を師に迎え、典籍に通じていたことから長男相続説を守っておられており、応神天皇崩御後に兄の大鶴鷯命に皇位に就くよう勧められた。しかし、大鶴鷯命がこれを拒否したことから皇位の譲り合いが三年程続いた。その間、菟道稚郎子命は宮居を菟道に移され、最終的に皇位を早く定めて天下の煩いを除くためにと自害され、大鶴鷯命に皇位を譲られたのである宇治神社の案内看板より。。 *: 本殿には、菟道稚郎子像と伝える神像（国の[[重要文化財]]）が祀られている。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]で「宇治神社二座」と見える2座のうち1座に比定される。この「二座」を祭神と見た場合、菟道稚郎子を1座とすることは動かないものの、もう1座については父の応神天皇・異母兄の仁徳天皇・母の矢河枝比売とする諸説がある『日本の神々』宇治上神社・宇治神社項。。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 創建年代などの起源は明らかではない。宇治神社のすぐ近くには[[宇治上神社]]があるが（{{Coord|34|53|31.44|N|135|48|41.18|E|region:JP-26|name=宇治上神社}}）、宇治神社とは二社一体の存在であった。 宇治上神社は、第15代[[応神天皇|応身天皇]]の離宮「桐原日桁宮」の旧跡であり、皇子の[[菟道稚郎子]]命の宮居跡と伝えられており、菟道稚郎子命の薨御後に御神霊を祀ったのが始まりである。 宇治上神社の境内は『山城国風土記』に見える菟道稚郎子の離宮「桐原日桁宮」の旧跡であると伝え、両社旧称の「離宮明神」もそれに因むといわれる。 宇治上神社の境内には「天降石」・「岩神さん」と呼ばれる巨石があり、[[磐境]]信仰による創祀という説もある。 === 概史 === [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[山城国]][[宇治郡]]に「宇治神社二座 鍬靫」の記載があるが、その2座はそれぞれ宇治神社・宇治上神社に比定される『京都府の地名』宇治上神社項・宇治神社項。。神名帳の「鍬靫」の記載は、祈年祭の際に朝廷から鍬・靫の奉献があったことを意味する。後に近くに[[平等院]]ができると、両社はその[[鎮守社]]とされたという。 [[明治]]以前は宇治神社は「下社」・「若宮」、宇治上神社は「上社」・「本宮」と呼ばれたほか、両社を合わせて「宇治離宮明神」・「宇治離宮八幡宮」と総称された。 明治に入って宇治上神社とは分離し、[[1911年]]（明治44年）[[府社]]に昇格した。 [[2018年]]（[[平成]]30年）には[[平成30年台風第21号|台風第21号]]で大鳥居が土台部分から折れて倒壊する被害に遭った{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.mbs.jp/news/kansainews/20180905/GE000000000000024232.shtml|title=台風21号被害　京都・宇治神社で大鳥居が土台部分から折れる|date=2018-09-05|accessdate=2018-09-05|publisher=毎日放送|archivedate=2018-09-06|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20180906135346/https://www.mbs.jp/news/kansainews/20180905/GE000000000000024232.shtml}}。 === 神使いの兎 === 菟道稚郎子命が、河内の国から菟道へ向かわれる途中、道に迷われた。その際、一羽のうさぎが現れ、後からついてこられる命を先導したという古伝から「みかえりうさぎ」といわれ、神使とされている。 現在、宇治神社ではみかえり兎の絵馬やおみくじを頒布しており、毎月一日のみ、ブロンズのみかえりうさぎの置物が現れる{{Cite web|和書|url=http://uji-jinja.com/event/150403usagi.html |title=〜願掛け　うさぎさん巡り、「みかえり兎」おみくじ〜 |access-date=2023-08-06 |publisher=宇治神社}}。 == 境内 == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|本殿（重要文化財）}}]] * 本殿（[[重要文化財]]） - 三間社[[流造]]、[[檜皮葺]]。[[鎌倉時代]]後期の造営とされる。 * 中門 * 拝殿（桐原殿、京都府指定有形文化財） - 入母屋造、桁行3間、梁間3間、檜皮葺。 * 絵馬堂 * 神楽殿 * 神輿蔵 * 社務所 - 2階は参集殿になっている。 * 飛び地境内 ** [[御旅所]]（京都府宇治市宇治1、{{ウィキ座標|34|53|16.91|N|135|47|55.73|E|region:JP-26|位置|name=御旅所}}） File:Uji-jinja, keidai.jpg|境内
{{small|左奥に本殿、右に拝殿（桐原殿）。}} File:Uji-jinja, keidai-torii.jpg|中門前の鳥居 File:Uji-jinja, Kirihara-den.jpg|拝殿（桐原殿） == 摂末社 == * 春日社（京都府指定有形文化財） - 祭神：[[タケミカヅチ|建甕槌命]]、[[フツヌシ|斎主命]]、[[天児屋根命]]。室町時代築。 * 日吉神社（京都府指定有形文化財） - 祭神：[[大山咋命]] * 住吉神社（京都府指定有形文化財） - 祭神：[[住吉三神]]（底筒男命、中筒男命、上筒男命）、[[神功皇后]] * 廣田神社 - 祭神：[[ヒルコ|蛭子命]] * 松尾神社 - 祭神：[[市杵島姫命]] * 高良神社 - 祭神：[[武内宿禰]] * 伊勢両宮社 - 祭神：[[天照皇大神]]、[[国常立命]] ファイル:Uji-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|境内社
画像右から
春日神社

住吉神社 ファイル:Uji-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|境内社

== 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 本殿 1棟（建造物） *: [[鎌倉時代]]後期の造営。[[1902年]]（[[明治]]35年）7月31日に[[古社寺保存法]]に基づき特別保護建造物に指定、[[1950年]]（[[昭和]]25年）の[[文化財保護法]]施行により重要文化財に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|1949|宇治神社本殿}}。 * 木造莵道稚郎子命坐像 1軀（彫刻） *: [[平安時代]]の作。[[1903年]]（明治36年）4月15日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|3643|木造莵道稚郎子命坐像}}。 === 京都府指定有形文化財 === * 拝殿 1棟（建造物） * 末社春日社本殿 1棟（建造物） - [[2018年]]（[[平成]]30年）3月23日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_2965_1.pdf 平成30年3月23日京都府公報]}}より京都府教育委員会告示第3号（リンクは京都府ホームページ）。。 * 末社日吉社本殿 1棟（建造物） * 末社住吉社本殿 1棟（建造物） === 宇治市指定有形文化財 === * 白色尉面（雪掻きの面） 1面（彫刻） - [[安土桃山時代]]の作。[[1972年]]（[[昭和]]47年）3月1日指定[https://www.city.uji.kyoto.jp/0000004257.html 宇治市の文化財]（宇治市ホームページ）。。 * 木造狛犬 2躯（彫刻） - 鎌倉時代の作。宇治市歴史資料館に寄託。[[1988年]]（昭和63年）3月31日指定。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[京都府]][[宇治市]]宇治山田1 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道 ** [[京阪電気鉄道]]（京阪電鉄）[[京阪宇治線|宇治線]] [[宇治駅 (京阪)|宇治駅]] （徒歩5分） ** [[西日本旅客鉄道]]（JR西日本）[[JR奈良線|奈良線]] [[宇治駅 (JR西日本)|宇治駅]] （徒歩10分） '''周辺''' * [[宇治上神社]] * [[平等院]] * 菟道稚郎子尊 宇治墓 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『[[日本歴史地名大系]] 京都府の地名』（[[平凡社]]）宇治市 宇治上神社項・宇治神社項 * 山路興造「宇治上神社・宇治神社」（[[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 5 山城・近江』（[[白水社]]、1986年） ISBN 978-4560025055） == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Uji-jinja}} * [http://uji-jinja.com/ 宇治神社] - 公式サイト *[https://www.kyotoside.jp/entry/20210306 京都府公式メディア（宇治神社）] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うししんしや}} [[Category:宇治市の建築物]] [[Category:宇治市の歴史]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:宇治市の重要文化財]] [[Category:京都府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:京都府の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]] [[Category:琵琶湖国定公園]] [[Category:日本遺産]]

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = 101127 Uji-jinja Uji Kyoto pref Japan01s3.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.89106|135.81069|type:landmark_region:JP|format=dms|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | image = [[File:Uji-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Chūmon (Middle Gate) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ({{ill|Uji Shrine|ja|宇治神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|ja|京阪宇治線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = 101127 Uji-jinja Uji Kyoto pref Japan01s3.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.89106|135.81069|type:landmark_region:JP|format=dms|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | image = [[File:Uji-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Chūmon (Middle Gate) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ({{ill|Uji Shrine|ja|宇治神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|ja|京阪宇治線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{draft categories| }} 宇治神社 | image = 101127 Uji-jinja Uji Kyoto pref Japan01s3.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.89106|135.81069|type:landmark_region:JP|format=dms|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | image = [[File:Uji-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Chūmon (Middle Gate) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ({{ill|Uji Shrine|ja|宇治神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|ja|京阪宇治線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{translated page|ja|宇治神社|version=100017465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings in Uji City]] [[Category:History of Uji City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties]] [[Category:National Parks of Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uji Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uji Shrine]] [[Category:前賢故実]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Uji Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Uji Shrine]] [[ja:宇治神社]] [[en:Uji Shrine]] [[zh:宇治神社]] [[de:Uji-Schrein]] [[fr:Uji-jinja]] [[ro:Uji (sanctuar)]]

（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|ja|京阪宇治線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{translated Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine.
