revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = 101127 Uji-jinja Uji Kyoto pref Japan01s3.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.89106|135.81069|type:landmark_region:JP|format=dms|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | image = [[File:Uji-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Chūmon (Middle Gate) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ({{ill|Uji Shrine|ja|宇治神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実|1=Zenken Kojitsu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|ja|京阪宇治線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{translated page|ja|宇治神社|version=100017465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings in Uji City]] [[Category:History of Uji City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties]] [[Category:National Parks of Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Uji Shrine]][[Category:Uji Shrine]] [[sn:Uji Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uji Shrine]] [[ja:宇治神社]] [[en:Uji Shrine]] [[zh:宇治神社]] [[de:Uji-Schrein]] [[fr:Uji-jinja]] [[ro:Uji (sanctuar)]]

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = 101127 Uji-jinja Uji Kyoto pref Japan01s3.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.89106|135.81069|type:landmark_region:JP|format=dms|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | image = [[File:Uji-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Chūmon (Middle Gate) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wikicoordinates|qq=}}|34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ({{ill|Uji Shrine|ja|宇治神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami Shrine|qq=}}. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=}}
（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実|1=Zenken Kojitsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenken Kojitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenken Kojitsu|qq=}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami Shrine|qq=}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinjusha|qq=}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-in|qq=}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|ja|京阪宇治線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Uji Line|qq=}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Nara Line|qq=}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-in|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{translated page|ja|宇治神社|version=100017465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings in Uji City]] [[Category:History of Uji City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties]] [[Category:National Parks of Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Uji Shrine]][[Category:Uji Shrine]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[sn:Uji Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uji Shrine]] [[ja:宇治神社]] [[en:Uji Shrine]] [[zh:宇治神社]] [[de:Uji-Schrein]] [[fr:Uji-jinja]] [[ro:Uji (sanctuar)]]

In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|ja|京阪宇治線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Uji Line|qq=}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Nara Line|qq=}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-in|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{translated page|ja|宇治神社|version=100017465|comment=Imported full revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = 101127 Uji-jinja Uji Kyoto pref Japan01s3.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.89106|135.81069|type:landmark_region:JP|format=dms|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | image = [[File:Uji-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Chūmon (Middle Gate) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wikicoordinates|qq=}}|34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ({{ill|Uji Shrine|ja|宇治神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami Shrine|qq=}}. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=}}
（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実|1=Zenken Kojitsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenken Kojitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenken Kojitsu|qq=}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami Shrine|qq=}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinjusha|qq=}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-in|qq=}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|ja|京阪宇治線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Uji Line|qq=}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Nara Line|qq=}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-in|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{translated page|ja|宇治神社|version=100017465|comment=Imported full It forms a pair with the adjacent {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami Shrine|qq=}}. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=}}
（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実|1=Zenken Kojitsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenken Kojitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenken Kojitsu|qq=}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami Shrine|qq=}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinjusha|qq=}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-in|qq=}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|ja|京阪宇治線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Uji Line|qq=}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Nara Line|qq=}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-in|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{translated page|ja|宇治神社|version=100017465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings in Uji City]] [[Category:History of Uji City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties]] [[Category:National Parks of Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Uji Shrine]][[Category:Uji Shrine]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[sn:Uji Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uji Shrine]] [[ja:宇治神社]] [[en:Uji Shrine]] [[zh:宇治神社]] [[de:Uji-Schrein]] [[fr:Uji-jinja]] [[ro:Uji (sanctuar)]]

Chūmon (Middle Gate) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wikicoordinates|qq=}}|34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ({{ill|Uji Shrine|ja|宇治神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami Shrine|qq=}}. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=}}
（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実|1=Zenken Kojitsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenken Kojitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenken Kojitsu|qq=}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami Shrine|qq=}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinjusha|qq=}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-in|qq=}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|ja|京阪宇治線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Uji Line|qq=}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Nara Line|qq=}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-in|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{translated page|ja|宇治神社|version=100017465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings in Uji City]] [[Category:History of Uji City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[en:Uji Shrine]] [[zh:宇治神社]] [[de:Uji-Schrein]] [[fr:Uji-jinja]] [[ro:Uji (sanctuar)]]

revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = 101127 Uji-jinja Uji Kyoto pref Japan01s3.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.89106|135.81069|type:landmark_region:JP|format=dms|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | image = [[File:Uji-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Chūmon (Middle Gate) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wikicoordinates|qq=}}|34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ({{ill|Uji Shrine|ja|宇治神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Shrine|qq=}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami Shrine|qq=}}. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=}}
（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実|1=Zenken Kojitsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenken Kojitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenken Kojitsu|qq=}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami Shrine|qq=}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinjusha|qq=}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-in|qq=}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|ja|京阪宇治線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keihan Uji Line|qq=}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|JR Nara Line|ja|奈良線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Nara Line|qq=}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|ja|宇治上神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|ja|平等院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Byōdō-in|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{translated page|ja|宇治神社|version=100017465|comment=Imported full 宇治神社 | image = 101127 Uji-jinja Uji Kyoto pref Japan01s3.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.89106|135.81069|type:landmark_region:JP|format=dms|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | image = [[File:Uji-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Chūmon (Middle Gate) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wikicoordinates|qq=}}|34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ([[:en:Uji Shrine|Uji Shrine]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]]. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=}}
（from the ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]], believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] for the nearby [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]]. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Keihan Uji Line|Keihan Uji Line]] Uji Station * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] Uji Station == Nearby == * [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]] * [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{translated page|ja|宇治神社|version=100017465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings in Uji City]] [[Category:History of Uji City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto]] [[Category:National Parks of Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Uji Shrine]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[sn:Uji Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uji Shrine]] [[ja:宇治神社]] [[en:Uji Shrine]] [[zh:宇治神社]] [[de:Uji-Schrein]] [[fr:Uji-jinja]] [[ro:Uji (sanctuar)]] [[Category:京都府の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:宇治市の建築物]]

（from the ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]], believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] for the nearby [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]]. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Keihan Uji Line|Keihan Uji Line]] Uji Station * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] Uji Station == Nearby == * [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]] * [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{translated page|ja|宇治神社|version=100017465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings in Uji City]] [[Category:History of Uji City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto]] [[Category:National Parks of Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Uji Shrine]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[sn:Uji Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uji Shrine]] [[ja:宇治神社]] [[en:Uji Shrine]] [[zh:宇治神社]] [[de:Uji-Schrein]] [[fr:Uji-jinja]] [[ro:Uji (sanctuar)]] [[Category:京都府の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:宇治市の建築物]] Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = 101127 Uji-jinja Uji Kyoto pref Japan01s3.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.89106|135.81069|type:landmark_region:JP|format=dms|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | image = [[File:Uji-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Chūmon (Middle Gate) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wikicoordinates|qq=}}|34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ([[:en:Uji Shrine|Uji Shrine]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]]. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=}}
（from the ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]], believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] for the nearby [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]]. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Keihan Uji Line|Keihan Uji Line]] Uji Station * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] Uji Station == Nearby == * [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]] * [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = 101127 Uji-jinja Uji Kyoto pref Japan01s3.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.89106|135.81069|type:landmark_region:JP|format=dms|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | image = [[File:Uji-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Chūmon (Middle Gate) | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wikicoordinates|qq=}}|34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ([[:en:Uji Shrine|Uji Shrine]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]]. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=}}
（from the ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]], believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] for the nearby [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]]. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Keihan Uji Line|Keihan Uji Line]] Uji Station * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] Uji Station == Nearby == * [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]] * [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{translated page|ja|宇治神社|version=100017465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings in Uji City]] [[Category:History of Uji City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto]] [[Category:National Parks of Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Uji Shrine]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[sn:Uji Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uji Shrine]] [[ja:宇治神社]] [[en:Uji Shrine]] [[zh:宇治神社]] [[de:Uji-Schrein]] [[fr:Uji-jinja]] [[ro:Uji (sanctuar)]] [[Category:京都府の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Buildings in Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:宇治市の歴史]] [[Category:宇治市の重要文化財]] [[Category:日本遺産]] [[Category:Biwako Quasi-National Park]] [[Category:Architecture of Kamakura period]] [[Category:11th-century architecture]] {{Commons category|Uji-jinja}} {{Commons category|Uji-jinja}} {{Commons category|Uji-jinja}} {{Commons category|Uji-jinja}}

It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]]. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=}}
（from the ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]], believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] for the nearby [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]]. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Keihan Uji Line|Keihan Uji Line]] Uji Station * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] Uji Station == Nearby == * [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]] * [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{translated page|ja|宇治神社|version=100017465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings in Uji City]] [[Category:History of Uji City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto]] [[Category:National Parks of Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:Expanded pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Uji Shrine]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[sn:Uji Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uji Shrine]] [[ja:宇治神社]] [[en:Uji Shrine]] [[zh:宇治神社]] [[de:Uji-Schrein]] [[fr:Uji-jinja]] [[ro:Uji (sanctuar)]] [[Category:京都府の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Buildings in Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:宇治市の重要文化財]] [[Category:日本遺産]] [[Category:Biwako Quasi-National Park]] [[Category:Architecture of Kamakura period]] [[Category:11th-century It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]]. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=}}
（from the ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]], believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] for the nearby [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]]. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Keihan Uji Line|Keihan Uji Line]] Uji Station * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] Uji Station == Nearby == * [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]] * [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{translated page|ja|宇治神社|version=100017465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Uji City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto]] [[Category:National Parks of Lake Biwa]]
