It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]]. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=}}
（from the ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]], believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] for the nearby [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]]. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Keihan Uji Line|Keihan Uji Line]] Uji Station * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] Uji Station == Nearby == * [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]] * [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{translated page|ja|宇治神社|version=100017465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:National Parks of Lake Biwa]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Pages goodbye $1 Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Uji Shrine]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[sn:Uji Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uji Shrine]] [[ja:宇治神社]] [[en:Uji Shrine]] [[zh:宇治神社]] [[de:Uji-Schrein]] [[fr:Uji-jinja]] [[ro:Uji (sanctuar)]] It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]]. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=}}
（from the ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[Important Cultural Property]]). According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]], believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] for the nearby [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]]. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Keihan Uji Line|Keihan Uji Line]] Uji Station * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] Uji Station == Nearby == * [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]] * [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{translated page|ja|宇治神社|version=100017465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Uji Shrine]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[sn:Uji Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uji Shrine]] [[ja:宇治神社]] [[en:Uji Shrine]] [[zh:宇治神社]] [[de:Uji-Schrein]] [[fr:Uji-jinja]] [[ro:Uji (sanctuar)]] [[Category:京都府の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Buildings in Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:宇治市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Biwako Quasi-National Park]] [[Category:Architecture of Kamakura period]] [[Category:11th-century architecture]] {{Commons category|Uji-jinja}} {{Commons It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]]. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=}}
（from the ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]). According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]], believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] for the nearby [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]]. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Keihan Uji Line|Keihan Uji Line]] Uji Station * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] Uji Station == Nearby == * [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]] * [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{Commonscat-inline|Uji-jinja|Uji Shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Japan-religious-struct-stub}} {{translated page|ja|宇治神社|version=100017465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[sn:Uji Shrine]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[sn:Uji Shrine]]}} [[sn:Uji Shrine]] [[ja:宇治神社]] [[en:Uji Shrine]] [[zh:宇治神社]] Quasi-National Park]] [[Category:Architecture of Kamakura period]] [[Category:11th-century architecture]] {{Commons category|Uji-jinja}} {{Commons category|Uji-jinja}} {{Commons category|Uji-jinja}} It forms a pair with the adjacent [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]]. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[Uji, Kyoto|Uji]] in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=}}
（from the ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]). According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]], believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] for the nearby [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]]. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Keihan Uji Line|Keihan Uji Line]] Uji Station * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] Uji Station == Nearby == * [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]] * [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == of Uji, Kyoto]] [[Category:宇治市の Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Biwako Quasi-National Park]] [[Category:Architecture of Kamakura period]] [[Category:11th-century architecture]] {{Commons According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]], believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] for the nearby [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]]. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Keihan Uji Line|Keihan Uji Line]] Uji Station * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] Uji Station == Nearby == * [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]] * [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]]. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[:en:Uji|Uji]] in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=}}
（from the ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]). According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]], believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] for the nearby [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]]. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q12742788]] Wikidata name: Uji Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = 101127 Uji-jinja Uji Kyoto pref Japan01s3.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34.89106|135.81069|type:landmark_region:JP|format=dms|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | image = [[File:Uji-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Chūmon (Middle Gate) | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 | coordinates = {{ill|Wikicoordinates|en|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wikicoordinates|qq=}}|34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ([[:en:Uji Shrine|Uji Shrine]]) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]]. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[:en:Uji|Uji]] in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=}}
（from the ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]). According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]], believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] for the nearby [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]]. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Keihan Uji Line|Keihan Uji Line]] Uji Station * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] Uji Station == Nearby == * [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]] * [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q12742788]] Wikidata name: Uji Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = File:Uji-jinja-06.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.8911|N|135.811|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|qid=Q11619989}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 |34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 | location = {{ill|Uji|qid=Q326956}} }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ([[:en:Uji Shrine|Uji Shrine]]) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]]. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[:en:Uji|Uji]] in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}
（from the ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]). According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]], believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] for the nearby [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]]. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Keihan Uji Line|Keihan Uji Line]] Uji Station * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] Uji Station == Nearby == * [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]] * [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q12742788]] Wikidata name: Uji Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = File:Uji-jinja-06.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.8911|N|135.811|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|qid=Q11619989}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 |34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 | location = {{ill|Uji|qid=Q326956}} }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ([[:en:Uji Shrine|Uji Shrine]]) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]]. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[:en:Uji|Uji]] in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}
（from the ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]). According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]], believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] for the nearby [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]]. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Keihan Uji Line|Keihan Uji Line]] Uji Station * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] Uji Station == Nearby == * [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]] * [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q12742788]] Wikidata name: Uji Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = File:Uji-jinja-06.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.8911|N|135.811|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|qid=Q11619989}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 |34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 | location = {{ill|Uji|qid=Q326956}} }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ([[:en:Uji Shrine|Uji Shrine]]) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]]. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[:en:Uji|Uji]] in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}
（from the ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]). According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]], believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] for the nearby [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]]. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Keihan Uji Line|Keihan Uji Line]] Uji Station * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] Uji Station == Nearby == * [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]] * [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q12742788]] Wikidata name: Uji Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = File:Uji-jinja-06.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.8911|N|135.811|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|qid=Q11619989}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 |34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 | location = {{ill|Uji|qid=Q326956}} }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ([[:en:Uji Shrine|Uji Shrine]]) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]]. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[:en:Uji|Uji]] in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}
（from the ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]). According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]], believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] for the nearby [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]]. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Keihan Uji Line|Keihan Uji Line]] Uji Station * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] Uji Station == Nearby == * [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]] * [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == [[commons:Category:Uji-jinja]][[de:Uji-Schrein]][[en:Uji Shrine]][[fr:Uji-jinja]][[ja:宇治神社]][[ro:Uji (sanctuar)]][[zh:宇治神社]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q12742788]] Wikidata name: Uji Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = File:Uji-jinja-06.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.8911|N|135.811|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|qid=Q11619989}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 |34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 | location = {{ill|Uji|qid=Q326956}} }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ([[:en:Uji Shrine|Uji Shrine]]) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]]. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[:en:Uji|Uji]] in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|qq=|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}
（from the ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|qq=|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]). According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]], believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] for the nearby [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]]. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Keihan Uji Line|Keihan Uji Line]] Uji Station * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] Uji Station == Nearby == * [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]] * [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == [[sn:commons:Category:Uji-jinja]][[de:Uji-Schrein]][[en:Uji Shrine]][[fr:Uji-jinja]][[ja:宇治神社]][[ro:Uji (sanctuar)]][[zh:宇治神社]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q12742788]] Wikidata name: Uji Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = File:Uji-jinja-06.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.8911|N|135.811|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 |34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 | location = {{ill|Uji|WD=Q326956}} }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ([[:en:Uji Shrine|Uji Shrine]]) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]]. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[:en:Uji|Uji]] in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}
（from the ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]). According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]], believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] for the nearby [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]]. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Keihan Uji Line|Keihan Uji Line]] Uji Station * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] Uji Station == Nearby == * [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]] * [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == [[sn:commons:Category:Uji-jinja]][[de:Uji-Schrein]][[en:Uji Shrine]][[fr:Uji-jinja]][[ja:宇治神社]][[ro:Uji (sanctuar)]][[zh:宇治神社]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q12742788]] Wikidata name: Uji Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = File:Uji-jinja-06.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.8911|N|135.811|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 |34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 | location = {{ill|Uji|WD=Q326956}} }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ([[:en:Uji Shrine|Uji Shrine]]) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Uji City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]]. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of [[:en:Uji|Uji]] in [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}
（from the ''[[:en:Zenken Kojitsu|Zenken Kojitsu]]''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|1=Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property (Japan)]]). According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]], believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] for the nearby [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]]. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it follows the three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from [[:en:Keihan Uji Line|Keihan Uji Line]] Uji Station * 10-minute walk from [[:en:Nara Line (JR West)|JR Nara Line]] Uji Station == Nearby == * [[:en:Ujigami Shrine|Ujigami Shrine]] * [[:en:Byōdō-in|Byōdō-in]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links ==
