（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|WD=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu|1=Zenken Kojitsu}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|1=Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. It forms a pair with the adjacent {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|en|Ujigami Shrine|WD=Q583589|lt=Ujigami Shrine|1=Ujigami Shrine}}. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji|1=Uji}} in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|1=Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}
（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|WD=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu|1=Zenken Kojitsu}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|1=Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|en|Ujigami Shrine|WD=Q583589|lt=Ujigami Shrine|1=Ujigami Shrine}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=Chinjusha|1=Chinjusha}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|en|Byōdō-in|WD=Q61094|lt=Byōdō-in|1=Byōdō-in}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|en|Keihan Uji Line|WD=Q1702750|lt=Keihan Uji Line|1=Keihan Uji Line}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|en|Nara Line (JR West)|WD=Q1157044|lt=JR Nara Line|1=Nara Line (JR West)}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|en|Ujigami Shrine|WD=Q583589|lt=Ujigami Shrine|1=Ujigami Shrine}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|en|Byōdō-in|WD=Q61094|lt=Byōdō-in|1=Byōdō-in}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == （from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|WD=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu|1=Zenken Kojitsu}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|1=Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural [[co:commons:Category:Uji-jinja]][[de:Uji-Schrein]][[en:Uji Shrine]][[fr:Uji-jinja]][[ja:宇治神社]][[ro:Uji (sanctuar)]][[zh:宇治神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Uji-jinja]][[de:Uji-Schrein]][[en:Uji Shrine]][[fr:Uji-jinja]][[ja:宇治神社]][[ro:Uji (sanctuar)]][[zh:宇治神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Uji-jinja]][[de:Uji-Schrein]][[en:Uji Shrine]][[fr:Uji-jinja]][[ja:宇治神社]][[ro:Uji (sanctuar)]][[zh:宇治神社]] { {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = File:Uji-jinja-06.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.8911|N|135.811|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989|1=Uji no Wakiiratsuko}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 |34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|1=Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 | location = {{ill|Uji|WD=Q326956|1=Uji}} }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ({{ill|Uji Shrine|en|Uji Shrine|WD=Q12742788|lt=Uji Shrine|1=Uji Shrine}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Uji City]], {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|en|Ujigami Shrine|WD=Q583589|lt=Ujigami Shrine|1=Ujigami Shrine}}. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji|1=Uji}} in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|1=Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}
（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|WD=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu|1=Zenken Kojitsu}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|1=Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|en|Ujigami Shrine|WD=Q583589|lt=Ujigami Shrine|1=Ujigami Shrine}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=Chinjusha|1=Chinjusha}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|en|Byōdō-in|WD=Q61094|lt=Byōdō-in|1=Byōdō-in}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|en|Keihan Uji Line|WD=Q1702750|lt=Keihan Uji Line|1=Keihan Uji Line}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|en|Nara Line (JR West)|WD=Q1157044|lt=JR Nara Line|1=Nara Line (JR West)}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|en|Ujigami Shrine|WD=Q583589|lt=Ujigami Shrine|1=Ujigami Shrine}} * {{ill|Byōdō-in|en|Byōdō-in|WD=Q61094|lt=Byōdō-in|1=Byōdō-in}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://uji-jinja.com/ Official Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} == External links == { {{Unreferenced|date=August 2020}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct}}{{Infobox religious building | name = Uji Shrine
宇治神社 | image = File:Uji-jinja-06.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.8911|N|135.811|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989|1=Uji no Wakiiratsuko}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | name = Uji Shrine | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 |34|53|27.77|N|135|48|38.52|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uji Shrine | deities = {{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|1=Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}} | Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), Former Prefectural Shrine | main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (Three-bay style) | festival = May 8, June 8 | location = {{ill|Uji|WD=Q326956|1=Uji}} }} [[File:Uji-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Uji Shrine''' ({{ill|Uji Shrine|en|Uji Shrine|WD=Q12742788|lt=Uji Shrine|1=Uji Shrine}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Uji City]], {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a designated {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]]. It forms a pair with the adjacent {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|en|Ujigami Shrine|WD=Q583589|lt=Ujigami Shrine|1=Ujigami Shrine}}. {{Nihongo|'''Uji Shrine'''|宇治神社|Uji-jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in the city of {{ill|Uji|en|Uji|WD=Q326956|lt=Uji|1=Uji}} in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is adjacent to the [[Ujigami Shrine]]. == Deities == [[File:莵道稚郎子.jpg|120px|thumb|right|{{center|{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko|1=Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}
（from the ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|WD=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu|1=Zenken Kojitsu}}''）}}]] The shrine's primary deity is: * '''{{ill|Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|ja|菟道稚郎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto|1=Uji no Wakiiratsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619989}}''' (Uji no Waki Iratsuko no Mikoto) ** Known as "菟道稚郎子" in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' and "宇遅之和紀郎子" in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'', he was the son of Emperor Ōjin. ** Emperor Ōjin designated Uji no Waki Iratsuko as his heir, assigning his elder brother, Ōsazaki no Mikoto (later Emperor Nintoku), as his guardian. Following Emperor Ōjin's death, Uji no Waki Iratsuko moved his residence to Uji. After prolonged disputes over succession, Uji no Waki Iratsuko eventually committed suicide to resolve the issue, passing the throne to his brother. ** The main hall houses a deity statue believed to be of Uji no Waki Iratsuko (a national {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}). According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}'', the shrine is one of "Uji Shrine, two seats." The second deity may be Emperor Ōjin, Emperor Nintoku, or their consort Yahata Hime, though interpretations vary. == History == === Establishment === The exact origins are unknown. Nearby {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|en|Ujigami Shrine|WD=Q583589|lt=Ujigami Shrine|1=Ujigami Shrine}}, believed to have been established on the site of the imperial residence of Emperor Ōjin, is closely connected to Uji Shrine. Together, they are known as the "Shrines of the Detached Palace." === Historical Overview === The shrine appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' as "Uji Shrine, two seats," with records of tools like plows and quivers being offered during prayer festivals. It later became a {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=Chinjusha|1=Chinjusha}} for the nearby {{ill|Byōdō-in|en|Byōdō-in|WD=Q61094|lt=Byōdō-in|1=Byōdō-in}}. In the Meiji era, Uji Shrine was separated from Ujigami Shrine and elevated to Prefectural Shrine status in 1911. In 2018, Typhoon Jebi caused significant damage, toppling the large torii at its entrance. === Myth of the Rabbit Guide === According to tradition, when Uji no Waki Iratsuko became lost on his journey, a rabbit appeared and guided him, earning the shrine's rabbit motif. The "Mikaeri Rabbit" is featured on votive plaques and charms, including a bronze figure displayed monthly. == Grounds == [[File:Uji-jinja, honden-1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)}}]] * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Built in the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, it follows the three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} style. * Worship Hall (Kirihara-den) - Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. * Other structures: Kagura Hall, Treasure Hall, and Office. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Main Hall * Wooden Seated Statue of Uji no Waki Iratsuko === Kyoto Prefectural Tangible Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall * Kasuga Shrine Main Hall == Events == The shrine holds several festivals, including: * May 8: festival * June 8: Return Festival (Rikyū Festival) == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[Uji City]], Ujiyamada 1 '''Transportation:''' * 5-minute walk from {{ill|Keihan Uji Line|en|Keihan Uji Line|WD=Q1702750|lt=Keihan Uji Line|1=Keihan Uji Line}} Uji Station * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Nara Line (JR West)|en|Nara Line (JR West)|WD=Q1157044|lt=JR Nara Line|1=Nara Line (JR West)}} Uji Station == Nearby == * {{ill|Ujigami Shrine|en|Ujigami Shrine|WD=Q583589|lt=Ujigami Shrine|1=Ujigami Shrine}} *
